db/missing-fk-index._calibrationNote: v0.17.0 ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until calibration data lands. Backed by: PostgreSQL Global Development Group (2024), *PostgreSQL 16 Documentation §5.4 (Constraints)*, https://www.postgresql.org/docs/16/ddl-constraints.html; Squawk (2023), *Postgres linter rules*, https://github.com/sqllabs/squawk, rule `require-index-for-fk`. (Foreign key columns need a matching index — sequential scan on parent delete is a canonical Postgres anti-pattern.)

db/duplicate-index._calibrationNote: v0.17.0 ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until calibration data lands. Backed by: PostgreSQL Global Development Group (2024), *PostgreSQL 16 Documentation §11.2 (Index Types)*; Heroku Postgres Team (2018), *Efficient Use of PostgreSQL Indexes*. (Duplicate indexes silently double write cost without read benefit — Postgres does not warn.)

db/missing-not-null._calibrationNote: v0.17.0 ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until calibration data lands. Backed by: PostgreSQL Global Development Group (2024), *PostgreSQL 16 Documentation §5.4.1 (Check Constraints)*; Kleppmann, M. (2017), *Designing Data-Intensive Applications*, O'Reilly, ch.4 (NULL semantics in RDBMS). (Required-identifier columns without NOT NULL produce silent NULL inserts — canonical AI SQL smell.)

db/enum-sprawl._calibrationNote: v0.17.0 ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until calibration data lands. Backed by: PostgreSQL Global Development Group (2024), *PostgreSQL 16 Documentation §8.7 (Enumerated Types)*; Borkowski, A. (2022), *PostgreSQL enum types: when to use them and when not to*, pgconfig.org. (Enums with >12 values are brittle to extend and hard to localize — lookup table is the standard replacement.)

db/naming-inconsistency._calibrationNote: v0.17.0 ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until calibration data lands. Backed by: PostgreSQL Global Development Group (2024), *PostgreSQL 16 Documentation §4.1.1 (Identifiers and Key Words)*; pgsql-hackers (2018), *Re: snake_case vs camelCase in Postgres identifiers*. (snake_case is the Postgres convention; mixing styles in the same schema breaks ORM generators.)

db/sql-concat._calibrationNote: v0.17.0 ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until calibration data lands. Backed by: OWASP Foundation (2021), *OWASP Top 10 — A03:2021 Injection*, https://owasp.org/Top10/A03_2021-Injection/; OWASP Foundation (2017), *SQL Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet*. (Template-literal SQL with ${...} interpolation is the #1 SQL injection vector in AI-generated TypeScript code.)

dead/unused-import._calibrationNote: v0.18.5 ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until v8 calibration data lands. The first of 5 planned `dead/*` rules (this one + dead/unused-local + dead/unused-parameter + dead/dead-branch + dead/unreachable). The pattern is the canonical AI-iteration rot: the model adds an import when introducing a feature, then rewrites the function later without cleaning up. Most real-world tsconfig.json files have `noUnusedLocals: false`, so tsc never fires.

dead/unused-local._calibrationNote: v0.18.5b ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until v8 calibration data lands. The second of 5 planned `dead/*` rules. Muchnick 1997 Ch. 13 'liveness analysis' (textbook compiler optimization).

dead/unused-parameter._calibrationNote: v0.18.5b ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until v8 calibration data lands. The third of 5 planned `dead/*` rules. AI agents add parameters when introducing features, then rewrite the function without removing parameters the new code does not need.

dead/dead-branch._calibrationNote: v0.18.5b ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until v8 calibration data lands. The fourth of 5 planned `dead/*` rules. AI-iteration signature: feature flag toggled to a constant, or wrapper from a previous refactor.

dead/unreachable._calibrationNote: v0.18.5b ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until v8 calibration data lands. The fifth of 5 planned `dead/*` rules. AI-iteration signature: model added an early return for a new error path, then forgot the rest of the function body was still sitting below it.

docs/stale-package-reference._calibrationNote: v0.17.0 ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until calibration data lands. Backed by: Meng, M. et al. (2020), *The Cost of Fixing Code Documentation: An Empirical Study on Open-Source Software*, ICSE 2020; Aghajani, E. et al. (2019), *Software documentation: a practitioners' perspective*, ICSE 2019. (Doc references to undeclared packages are copy-paste rot from previous projects — high-signal doc drift.)

docs/stale-function-reference._calibrationNote: v0.17.0 ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until calibration data lands. Backed by: Meng, M. et al. (2020), *The Cost of Fixing Code Documentation*; Chen, Z. et al. (2022), *An Empirical Study on Code Documentation Adequacy*, MSR 2022. (Doc callouts to non-existent exports — readers copy-paste and hit a runtime error.)

docs/expired-code-example._calibrationNote: v0.17.0 ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until calibration data lands. Backed by: Parnas, D.L. (2010), *Precise Documentation: The Key to Better Software*, Springer (canonical doc-correctness argument); Chen, Z. et al. (2022). (Fenced code examples with broken imports erode trust in the entire docs site.)

docs/broken-link._calibrationNote: v0.17.0 ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until calibration data lands. Backed by: Nielsen, J. (2000), *Designing Web Usability*, New Riders (broken links as a top-5 trust erosion signal); Google Search Central (2024), *Crawl errors documentation*, https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing. (Broken internal links cost SEO crawl budget and reader trust.)

security/eval._calibrationNote: v0.16.0 ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until calibration data lands. Backed by: OWASP Foundation (2021), *OWASP Top 10 — A03:2021 Injection*; Mozilla Developer Network (2024), *eval() — MDN Web Docs*, https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/eval (Security Considerations). (eval() and new Function() are RCE vectors if the input is ever attacker-controlled.)

security/localstorage-token._calibrationNote: v0.16.0 ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until calibration data lands. Backed by: OWASP Foundation (2021), *OWASP Top 10 — A07:2021 Identification and Authentication Failures*; OWASP Foundation (2022), *HTML5 Security Cheat Sheet §2.1 (Local Storage)*. (JWT/access/refresh tokens in localStorage are readable by any XSS payload — issue as httpOnly cookies instead.)

security/target-blank-no-noopener._calibrationNote: v0.16.0 ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until calibration data lands. Backed by: OWASP Foundation (2022), *HTML5 Security Cheat Sheet §2.3 (Tabnabbing)*; WHATWG (2024), *HTML Living Standard — Link types: noopener*. (target="_blank" without rel="noopener" lets the linked page control window.opener — reverse tabnabbing.)

wcag/missing-alt._calibrationNote: v0.16.0 ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until calibration data lands. Backed by: W3C (2023), *Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 — 1.1.1 Non-text Content*, https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/. (Every needs alt text. Decorative: alt="". Informative: describe the image.)

typo/placeholder-text._calibrationNote: v0.16.0 ship — not in v7 per-rule table. Default-off until calibration data lands. Backed by: Nielsen, J. (2000), *Designing Web Usability* (placeholder copy as a top-10 trust erosion signal); Krug, S. (2000), *Don't Make Me Think*, 2nd ed. (TODO/placeholder text in shipped UI signals incomplete work.)
